title: 10 best herbal teas for hangover

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with hangover because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and soothing properties. The tea's active ingredients, such as silicic acid and flavonoids, work to neutralize the toxins released by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue. Additionally, the tea's calming effects help to ease nausea and irritability, allowing for a quicker recovery from a night of indulgence.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory properties may help improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain and other organs, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Liver-protective effects: Some studies suggest that ginkgo biloba extracts may have liver-protective effects, which could help mitigate liver damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea associated with hangovers.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce headaches and body aches caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the leaves in a warm and dry environment away from direct sunlight for 24 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and drink immediately while still hot to alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with hangover because it contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms the mind and body. The soothing properties of chamomile tea can help to ease nausea and stomach discomfort often associated with a hangover. Additionally, its natural sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, allowing you to feel more refreshed and focused. By consuming chamomile tea after a night out, you can help alleviate the negative symptoms of a hangover and recover faster.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Chamomile tea contains antioxidants that help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which may help reduce inflammation in the body caused by binge drinking, alleviating symptoms like nausea, stomach upset, and joint pain.

GABA-boosting flavonoids: Chamomile tea contains flavonoids that can increase the production of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation. This can help alleviate hangover symptoms like anxiety, jitteriness, and insomnia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, chamomile roots help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with hangover.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of chamomile contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes headaches and relaxes the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers from a reputable supplier or store-bought packet measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Boil one cup of fresh water in a kettle or pot and let it cool for 5-7 minutes to avoid scalding the delicate herb.
Steep the dried chamomile flowers in the cooled water for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavonoids and terpenoids to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and retain the medicinal properties of the herb.
Serve the tea immediately while warm, ideally with a splash of lemon juice and honey if desired for added flavor and soothing relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with hangover because its natural oils can help alleviate symptoms of a pounding headache, nausea, and stomach discomfort. The menthol in peppermint tea acts as a natural coolant, soothing an irritated stomach and relaxing the muscles to reduce cramping. Additionally, peppermint's calming properties may help reduce anxiety and stress caused by a night of heavy drinking, promoting a sense of calm and clarity. Overall, herbal peppermint teas can be a gentle and effective way to alleviate hangover symptoms without harsh chemicals or added sugars.
remedy_properties: Digestive soothing: Peppermint oil's natural anti-inflammatory compounds can help calm nausea and soothe digestive discomfort caused by excessive drinking.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in peppermint tea may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Stomach acid reduction: Peppermint oil's menthol content has been shown to relax the stomach muscles, reducing the production of stomach acid that can exacerbate nausea, heartburn, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain menthol, which helps to ease nausea and stomach discomfort caused by hangovers.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves or one teaspoon of fresh peppermint leaves.
Heat filtered water to a boiling point and pour it over the measured peppermint leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the hot tea immediately and drink slowly over a period of 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with hangover because its unique blend of herbs, including passionflower and skullcap, work together to soothe the nervous system and calm inflammation in the body. The calming effects of maypop tea can help reduce anxiety and irritability associated with a hangover, while its anti-inflammatory properties can ease headaches and other symptoms caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, maypop tea's natural diuretic properties can aid in flushing out toxins from the body, leaving you feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant-rich flavonoids: Maypop tea's flavonoid content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which contributes to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of Maypop tea, including its caffeic acid and quercetin content, can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort often experienced during hangovers.
Digestive soothers: Maypop tea's natural relaxants, such as passionflower flavonoids, may help calm digestive issues like diarrhea, bloating, and cramps commonly associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Maypop are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Fruits: Maypop fruits contain vitamin C and other nutrients that help replenish the body's stores after a night of heavy drinking, reducing the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried maypop flowers and leaves from a reputable source.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes to ensure proper temperature.
Steep the maypop mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup with a spoon and discard solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool for 1-2 minutes before consuming within 30 minutes of hangover onset.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with hangover because of its calming and soothing properties, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The herb contains compounds like valerenic acid that interact with GABA receptors in the brain to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This can help mitigate the stress and discomfort caused by overindulgence, allowing the body to recover more quickly. Additionally, valerian tea's gentle sedative effects may even promote a restful night's sleep, helping to recharge and rejuvenate the body after a long night of celebration.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing compounds: Valerenic acid, a primary component of valerian root, has sedative and relaxing effects that can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the liver and other tissues caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like nausea and fatigue.
Digestive soothing effects: The volatile oils present in valerian root have been shown to soothe digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and stomach cramps, which are common hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, which help to alleviate symptoms of hangovers such as headaches and nausea.
Rhyzomes: Contain sedative compounds that can calm the stomach and reduce inflammation caused by excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis roots and bring 2 cups of water to boil in a medium saucepan.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discard solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well, let it sit for 2-3 minutes before drinking.
Drink the valerian tea slowly while keeping hydrated with water throughout the day, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with hangover because it contains compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce pain and discomfort in the body. Additionally, licorice root has natural antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid and relieve digestive issues often experienced after a night of heavy drinking. By soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation, licorice teas may help promote a faster recovery from a hangover.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by soothing the digestive tract.
Licoricidin: This component has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nausea effects, making it effective in reducing hangover-related pain and discomfort.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body after excessive alcohol consumption, potentially alleviating hangover symptoms like fatigue and brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to calm nausea and stomach upset often associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhizae glabra root and wash it thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the washed root into small pieces and steep one teaspoon of the herb in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Combine the strained liquid with cooled boiled water to make a total volume of 8 ounces and add honey to taste.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature and serve it chilled, ready for immediate consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng teas helps with hangover because it contains adaptogenic properties that help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The tea's antioxidant-rich composition also works to neutralize free radicals produced during excessive drinking, which can cause oxidative stress and contribute to hangover severity. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to improve mental clarity and reduce inflammation, helping to restore a sense of balance and well-being after a night of heavy drinking. By promoting healthy digestion and circulation, ginseng teas can also help to mitigate the discomfort and regret often associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng helps to regulate stress hormones like cortisol, which can become imbalanced after heavy drinking and exacerbate hangover symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals produced by alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hangover symptoms.
Glycyrrhizin content: The glycyrrhizin in ginseng has anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to alleviate headache, nausea, and other discomforts associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, a compound that helps alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce hangover-related headaches and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including one teaspoon of dried ginseng root.
Measure one cup of boiling water and steep the ginseng root for ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste and stir well to combine with the ginseng tea mixture.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoy within thirty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with hangover because it is a natural diuretic, which aids in flushing out toxins from the body. The tea's high water content helps replenish lost fluids and electrolytes, reducing dehydration and alleviating headaches. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an upset stomach, while its bitter taste stimulates digestion and absorption of nutrients. As a result, dandelion teas can provide relief from the unpleasant symptoms associated with hangover, allowing for a faster recovery and a smoother return to normal activities.
remedy_properties: Laxative properties: Dandelion tea helps to stimulate bowel movements, which can relieve hangover-related digestive issues such as constipation, bloating, and nausea.

Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea increases urine production, which can help to flush out excess toxins and alcohol from the body, reducing dehydration and headache symptoms associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hangover-related fatigue, headaches, and muscle aches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bitter compounds that help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that soothe headaches, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried dandelion roots and flowers from a trusted source.
Measure one teaspoon of dried dandelion root and two teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured herbs in a large pot with boiling water for five minutes to allow steeping.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-resistant pitcher or individual cups after the steeping process.
Refrigerate the cooled tea for at least thirty minutes before serving and consuming within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with hangover because it is a natural remedy that can soothe an upset stomach, calm nausea, and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants and volatile oils present in lemon balm tea help to neutralize the body's response to toxins, making it easier to digest and eliminating waste more efficiently. Additionally, its calming effects on the nervous system can alleviate headaches and promote a sense of relaxation, helping to ease the overall discomfort associated with hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants help neutralize the free radicals caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Calming effects: Lemon balm's calming compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea and headaches associated with hangovers, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Digestive soothing properties: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate digestive discomfort, bloating, and cramps caused by alcohol consumption, allowing the body to recover more quickly.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which help alleviate headaches and nausea associated with hangovers.
Flowers: Containing rosmarinic acid, a natural antihistamine that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried lemon balm leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes allowing the flavors and nutrients to combine.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh strainer or piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea while still warm or at room temperature within one hour of consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with hangover because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that can help alleviate symptoms of overindulgence. This herbal tea has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and depression, but its antihistamine properties make it an effective remedy for hangover headaches, nausea, and fatigue. As the tea is absorbed into the bloodstream, it helps to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, allowing the body to recover faster from the previous night's excesses.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: St. John's Wort contains a variety of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea.

Adaptogenic effects: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to promote mental clarity, reduce stress, and improve mood, which can be beneficial in alleviating the psychological symptoms of a hangover, such as irritability, anxiety, and depression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are rich in bioactive compounds that can help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Leaves: The leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help counteract the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, making them a popular choice for hangover remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves or purchase dried material from a reputable source measuring two teaspoons per cup of water.
Combine the st john's wort with boiling water in a teapot or mug steeping for five to seven minutes depending on personal preference.
Strain the tea into another container discarding the solids and adding honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature approximately ten to fifteen minutes before serving.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container refrigerated for up to twenty-four hours or frozen for later use.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for hangover are a natural remedy that can provide relief from symptoms associated with excessive alcohol consumption. These teas work by promoting digestion, reducing inflammation, and replenishing lost electrolytes and fluids. For example, peppermint tea helps alleviate nausea and headaches, while ginger tea reduces stomach upset and dizziness. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing a gentle and soothing way to recover from a hangover, allowing individuals to feel more refreshed and rejuvenated for the day ahead.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hangover with herbal teas is by sipping on soothing blends that calm the stomach and replenish lost fluids. Peppermint tea helps ease nausea and digestive discomfort, while ginger tea reduces inflammation and aids in digestion. Dandelion root tea stimulates liver function and promotes detoxification, while chamomile tea calms the mind and body. Brew a combination of these teas for a natural remedy that will help alleviate hangover symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal teas are various digestive issues, such as indigestion, bloating, and nausea. Ginger tea is commonly used to alleviate these symptoms, while peppermint tea can help soothe an upset stomach. Dandelion root tea may also be consumed to aid digestion and relieve mild headaches. Additionally, some herbs like passionflower and lemon balm are known for their calming properties, which can help with anxiety and stress, similar to the relief sought from a hangover remedy.
